2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# mega_loop.sh — wide-funnel driver for the lua-rs-port stub frontier.
3	#
4	# Instead of stair-stepping one test program at a time, this:
5	#   1. Runs N test programs in parallel-ish (sequentially with timeouts)
6	#   2. Collects the union of `not yet implemented:` panics
7	#   3. Drains the resulting stub queue, dispatching agents (one per
8	#      unique stub) — the agent prompt already invites family expansion,
9	#      so each dispatch typically clears 5-15 sibling stubs in one go
10	#   4. Re-runs all programs after the queue drains, finds any new
11	#      surfaced stubs, repeats
12	#   5. Stops when all programs pass OR a stuck condition fires
13	#
14	# Usage:
15	#   nohup ./harness/mega_loop.sh > /tmp/mega.log 2>&1 &
16	#
17	# Programs source (in priority order):
18	#   1. argv if given:   ./harness/mega_loop.sh prog1 prog2 ...
19	#   2. $TEST_PROGS_FILE env (newline-separated, # comments OK)
20	#   3. Built-in default list (see DEFAULT_PROGS below)
21	
22	set -uo pipefail
23	
24	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
25	cd "$ROOT"
26	
27	OUT_DIR="harness/impl"
28	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
29	LOG="$OUT_DIR/mega.log"
30	QUEUE="$OUT_DIR/stub_queue.txt"
31	touch "$LOG" "$QUEUE"
32	
33	MAX_OUTER=${MAX_OUTER:-20}
34	MAX_PER_OUTER=${MAX_PER_OUTER:-25}
35	PER_AGENT_BUDGET=${PER_AGENT_BUDGET:-20.00}
36	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-20}
37	SESSION_BUDGET=${SESSION_BUDGET:-400.00}
38	AUTO_COMMIT=${AUTO_COMMIT:-1}
39	COMMIT_LOCK_DIR="$OUT_DIR/commit.lock"
40	PREV_PASS_COUNT=-1
41	declare -a STUCK_PROGS=()
42	
43	# A frontier of Lua test programs. Each entry is a TSV: program\texpected_stdout.
44	# expected_stdout uses literal \n for newlines and \t for tabs (interpreted via printf %b).
45	# Empty expected_stdout = "anything non-error counts as a pass".
46	DEFAULT_PROGS=(
47	    $'print("hello")\thello'
48	    $'print(1+2)\t3'
49	    $'local x = 5; print(x)\t5'
50	    $'local x = 5; local y = 3; print(x+y)\t8'
51	    $'if 1 < 2 then print("yes") end\tyes'
52	    $'for i=1,3 do print(i) end\t1\n2\n3'
53	    $'local i = 1; while i <= 3 do print(i); i = i + 1 end\t1\n2\n3'
54	    $'local function f(x) return x*2 end; print(f(5))\t10'
55	    $'local function fact(n) if n <= 1 then return 1 else return n * fact(n-1) end end; print(fact(5))\t120'
56	    $'local function counter() local n = 0; return function() n = n + 1; return n end end; local c = counter(); print(c()); print(c())\t1\n2'
57	    $'local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])\t10'
58	    $'local t = {}; table.insert(t, "a"); print(t[1])\ta'
59	    $'print("hi " .. "there")\thi there'
60	    $'print(#"hello")\t5'
61	    $'print(string.upper("hello"))\tHELLO'
62	    $'print(string.format("%d", 42))\t42'
63	    $'print(math.sqrt(16))\t4.0'
64	    $'local ok, err = pcall(function() error("boom") end); print(ok)\tfalse'
65	    $'local a, b = (function() return 1, 2 end)(); print(a, b)\t1\t2'
66	    $'local t = {1,2,3}; local s = 0; for i=1,#t do s = s + t[i] end; print(s)\t6'
67	    $'local f = function(x, y) return x or y end; print(f(nil, 42))\t42'
68	    $'local t = {}; for i=1,3 do t[#t+1] = i*i end; for i=1,#t do print(t[i]) end\t1\n4\n9'
69	    $'local o = {x=10}; function o.get() return o.x end; print(o.get())\t10'
70	    $'local s = ""; for i=1,3 do s = s .. tostring(i) end; print(s)\t123'
71	    $'local function fib(n) if n < 2 then return n else return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2) end end; print(fib(10))\t55'
72	    # --- next wave: method syntax, varargs, ipairs/pairs, metatables, more stdlib ---
73	    $'local o = {x=10}; function o:get() return self.x end; print(o:get())\t10'
74	    $'local function sum(...) local s = 0; for _, v in ipairs({...}) do s = s + v end; return s end; print(sum(1,2,3,4,5))\t15'
75	    $'local t = {a=1, b=2, c=3}; local total = 0; for k,v in pairs(t) do total = total + v end; print(total)\t6'
76	    $'local mt = {__add = function(a, b) return {v = a.v + b.v} end}; local a = setmetatable({v=10}, mt); local b = setmetatable({v=5}, mt); print((a+b).v)\t15'
77	    $'print(string.sub("hello world", 7))\tworld'
78	    $'print(string.rep("ab", 3))\tababab'
79	    $'print(string.find("hello world", "world"))\t7\t11'
80	    $'print(math.max(1, 5, 3, 7, 2))\t7'
81	    $'print(math.floor(3.7))\t3'
82	    $'local t = {3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6}; table.sort(t); for i=1,#t do io.write(t[i]); if i < #t then io.write(",") end end; print()\t1,1,2,3,4,5,6,9'
83	    $'local function rev(s) local r = ""; for i = #s, 1, -1 do r = r .. string.sub(s, i, i) end; return r end; print(rev("hello"))\tolleh'
84	    $'local function compose(f, g) return function(x) return f(g(x)) end end; local addone = function(x) return x+1 end; local double = function(x) return x*2 end; print(compose(addone, double)(5))\t11'
85	    $'local function range(a, b) local t = {}; for i=a,b do t[#t+1] = i end; return t end; local r = range(1, 5); print(#r, r[1], r[5])\t5\t1\t5'
86	    # --- codegen gaps that block the official Lua test suite ---
87	    $'print(not true)\tfalse'
88	    $'print(not false)\ttrue'
89	    $'print(not nil)\ttrue'
90	    $'if not false then print("y") end\ty'
91	    $'local x = 1; if not (x == 2) then print("ne") end\tne'
92	    $'local r = (1==1 and 2==2 and 3==3); print(r)\ttrue'
93	    $'local r = (1==1 and 2==3 and 4==4); print(r)\tfalse'
94	    $'local function clip(x, lo, hi) if x < lo then return lo elseif x > hi then return hi else return x end end; print(clip(5,1,10), clip(-1,1,10), clip(99,1,10))\t5\t1\t10'
95	    $'do local i = 0; repeat i = i + 1; print(i) until i >= 3 end\t1\n2\n3'
96	    $'for i = 5, 1, -1 do print(i) end\t5\n4\n3\n2\n1'
97	    # --- official Lua 5.4 test files (sourced from reference/lua-c/testes) ---
98	    # No expected stdout: counted as a pass when binary exits with [ok] and no
99	    # panic/[err] — these tests typically self-assert with assert(...) calls.
100	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/tracegc.lua'
101	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/big.lua'
102	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/verybig.lua'
103	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/bwcoercion.lua'
104	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua'
105	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/heavy.lua'
106	    # These fail with a Phase-B stub blocker, so they feed the surface-scan
107	    # frontier and let one family-aware agent dispatch clear them.
108	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/goto.lua'
109	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/sort.lua'
110	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/closure.lua'
111	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/pm.lua'
112	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua'
113	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/bitwise.lua'
114	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/math.lua'
115	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/tpack.lua'
116	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua'
117	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/locals.lua'
118	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/calls.lua'
119	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/constructs.lua'
120	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/nextvar.lua'
121	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/utf8.lua'
122	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/files.lua'
123	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/errors.lua'
124	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/api.lua'
125	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/attrib.lua'
126	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/main.lua'
127	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/events.lua'
128	    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/code.lua'
129	)
130	
131	emit() {
132	    local ts; ts=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
133	    echo "[$ts mega] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"
134	}
135	
136	# Resolve which test programs to use. Each entry is TSV: prog\texpected.
137	# Split into two parallel arrays.
138	RAW=()
139	if [ $# -gt 0 ]; then
140	    RAW=("$@")
141	elif [ -n "${TEST_PROGS_FILE:-}" ] && [ -f "$TEST_PROGS_FILE" ]; then
142	    while IFS= read -r line; do
143	        [ -z "$line" ] && continue
144	        [[ "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
145	        RAW+=("$line")
146	    done < "$TEST_PROGS_FILE"
147	else
148	    RAW=("${DEFAULT_PROGS[@]}")
149	fi
150	
151	PREAMBLE='_soft = true
152	_port = true
153	_nomsg = true
154	_U = false
155	arg = arg or {}
156	_G = _G or _ENV
157	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
158	'
159	
160	PROGS=()
161	EXPECTED=()
162	LABELS=()
163	for entry in "${RAW[@]}"; do
164	    prog="${entry%%$'\t'*}"
165	    rest="${entry#*$'\t'}"
166	    if [ "$rest" = "$entry" ]; then
167	        exp=""
168	    else
169	        exp="$rest"
170	    fi
171	    label=""
172	    if [[ "$prog" == @FILE:* ]]; then
173	        path="${prog#@FILE:}"
174	        if [ ! -f "$path" ]; then
175	            emit "WARN: skipping @FILE entry — not found: $path"
176	            continue
177	        fi
178	        label="$path"
179	        prog="$PREAMBLE"$'\n'"$(cat "$path")"
180	    fi
181	    PROGS+=("$prog")
182	    EXPECTED+=("$exp")
183	    LABELS+=("$label")
184	done
185	
186	emit "loaded ${#PROGS[@]} test programs (with expected outputs)"
187	
188	# Make sure binary exists.
189	ensure_binary() {
190	    cargo build -q -p lua-cli >/dev/null 2>&1
191	}
192	
193	# Run one test program with timeout, return its output.
194	run_one() {
195	    local prog="$1"
196	    local out_file="$2"
197	    local bin="target/debug/lua-rs"
198	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
199	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1
200	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
201	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1
202	    else
203	        ( "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
204	        local pid=$!
205	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 $pid 2>/dev/null ) &
206	        local watcher=$!
207	        wait $pid 2>/dev/null
208	        kill $watcher 2>/dev/null
209	    fi
210	}
211	
212	# How many test runs to launch in parallel. lua-rs binary is single-threaded
213	# and each run is small; 8-16 is a sensible cap.
214	PARALLEL_RUNS=${PARALLEL_RUNS:-12}
215	# How many agent dispatches to run concurrently. Agents on the same file
216	# can conflict, so we serialize same-file work; this caps the *total*
217	# concurrent agents across different files.
218	PARALLEL_AGENTS=${PARALLEL_AGENTS:-3}
219	
220	# Run all programs in parallel into $1 dir; output files in $1/prog-N.out.
221	run_all_programs() {
222	    local scan_dir="$1"
223	    mkdir -p "$scan_dir"
224	    local pids=()
225	    local i=0
226	    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
227	        i=$((i + 1))
228	        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$i.out"
229	        while [ "${#pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_RUNS" ]; do
230	            local new_pids=()
231	            for p in "${pids[@]}"; do
232	                if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
233	            done
234	            pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
235	            [ "${#pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_RUNS" ] && sleep 0.2
236	        done
237	        ( run_one "$prog" "$out_file" || true ) &
238	        pids+=($!)
239	    done
240	    for p in "${pids[@]+"${pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done
241	}
242	
243	# Surface scan: derive unique stub blockers from a populated scan dir.
244	PARALLEL_RUNS=${PARALLEL_RUNS:-8}
245	surface_scan() {
246	    local scan_dir="$1"
247	    local stub_lines=()
248	    local idx=0
249	    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
250	        idx=$((idx + 1))
251	        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$idx.out"
252	        [ ! -f "$out_file" ] && continue
253	        local stub_func; stub_func=$(grep -E "not yet implemented: [a-z0-9_-]+:" "$out_file" \
254	            | head -1 | sed -E 's/^.*not yet implemented: [a-z0-9_-]+: //' \
255	            | sed -E 's/[[:space:]]+$//')
256	        if [ -n "$stub_func" ]; then
257	            local stub_loc; stub_loc=$(grep -oE "panicked at [^:]+:[0-9]+:[0-9]+" "$out_file" \
258	                | head -1 | sed 's/^panicked at //')
259	            stub_lines+=("$stub_func"$'\t'"$stub_loc")
260	        fi
261	    done
262	    if [ ${#stub_lines[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
263	        return 0
264	    fi
265	    printf "%s\n" "${stub_lines[@]}" | sort -u -t$'\t' -k1,1
266	}
267	
268	# Run all programs in parallel, return how many produce expected output.
269	# A program passes only when:
270	#   (a) exits cleanly ([ok] line + no panic + no [err]), AND
271	#   (b) if EXPECTED is non-empty, stdout (post-[4/4]) matches exactly.
272	count_passing() {
273	    local scan_dir="$1"
274	    local pass=0
275	    local idx=0
276	    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
277	        idx=$((idx + 1))
278	        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$idx.out"
279	        local exp="${EXPECTED[$((idx-1))]}"
280	        [ ! -f "$out_file" ] && continue
281	        if ! grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$out_file" \
282	            || grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$out_file" \
283	            || grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$out_file" \
284	            || grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
285	            continue
286	        fi
287	        if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
288	            local actual; actual=$(awk '
289	                /^\[4\/4\] Executing chunk/ { capture=1; next }
290	                /^\[ok\] execution completed/ { capture=0 }
291	                capture { print }
292	            ' "$out_file")
293	            local expected_decoded; expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
294	            if [ "$actual" = "$expected_decoded" ]; then
295	                pass=$((pass + 1))
296	            fi
297	        else
298	            pass=$((pass + 1))
299	        fi
300	    done
301	    echo "$pass"
302	}
303	
304	# Commit any pending changes under crates/ as one commit attributed to the
305	# named agent. Serialized via mkdir-lock so parallel agents don't race.
306	# Args: kind (impl|debug), target (func or prog), out_json (translator.json).
307	commit_agent_changes() {
308	    [ "$AUTO_COMMIT" != "1" ] && return 0
309	    local kind="$1"
310	    local target="$2"
311	    local out_json="$3"
312	
313	    local lock_tries=0
314	    while ! mkdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null; do
315	        lock_tries=$((lock_tries + 1))
316	        if [ "$lock_tries" -gt 120 ]; then
317	            emit "  commit-lock timeout, skipping commit for $kind/$target"
318	            return 0
319	        fi
320	        sleep 0.5
321	    done
322	
323	    if [ -z "$(git status --porcelain crates/ 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
324	        rmdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null
325	        return 0
326	    fi
327	
328	    local result_text; result_text=$(jq -r '.result // ""' "$out_json" 2>/dev/null)
329	    local title_target; title_target=$(echo "$target" | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-50)
330	    local subject="agent ${kind}: ${title_target}"
331	    local body_file="$OUT_DIR/.commit-body.tmp"
332	    {
333	        echo "$subject"
334	        echo
335	        printf "%s\n" "$result_text" | head -50
336	    } > "$body_file"
337	
338	    git add crates/ >/dev/null 2>&1
339	    if git commit -F "$body_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
340	        local sha; sha=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
341	        emit "  → committed $sha (${kind}/${title_target})"
342	    else
343	        emit "  → no commit (probably nothing in crates/)"
344	    fi
345	    rm -f "$body_file"
346	    rmdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null
347	}
348	
349	# Dispatch a family-aware implement agent for one stub (the prompt
350	# already invites the agent to handle siblings while in context).
351	dispatch_one() {
352	    local func="$1"
353	    local loc="${2:-}"
354	    local safe_name; safe_name=$(echo "$func" | tr -c 'A-Za-z0-9_' '_' | cut -c1-40)
355	    local out_json="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.translator.json"
356	    local transcript="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.transcript.jsonl"
357	
358	    local prompt_template; prompt_template=$(cat "$ROOT/harness/family_agent_prompt.txt")
359	    local prompt="${prompt_template//__FUNC__/$func}"
360	    prompt="${prompt//__LOC__/$loc}"
361	
362	    export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-personal"
363	    unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
364	    export CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS="${CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-64000}"
365	    export CLAUDE_TARGET_RS_FILE="crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs"
366	
367	    claude -p \
368	        --append-system-prompt "$(cat PORTING.md)" \
369	        --allowedTools "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash(cargo build*),Bash(cargo check*),Bash(grep *),Bash(rg *),Bash(cat *),Bash(head *),Bash(tail *),Bash(wc *),Bash(find *)" \
370	        --permission-mode dontAsk \
371	        --output-format stream-json \
372	        --include-partial-messages \
373	        --verbose \
374	        --max-budget-usd "$PER_AGENT_BUDGET" \
375	        "$prompt" \
376	        2>>"$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.stderr" \
377	        | tee "$transcript" >/dev/null
378	
379	    jq -s 'map(select(.type == "result")) | .[-1] // {}' "$transcript" > "$out_json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}' > "$out_json"
380	    local cost; cost=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$out_json")
381	    commit_agent_changes "impl" "$func" "$out_json"
382	    echo "$cost"
383	}
384	
385	# Dispatch a DEBUG agent for a failing test program (real error, not a stub).
386	# Mode "inline": $prog is Lua source. Mode "file": $prog is a path to a .lua file
387	# that the agent should run via ./harness/run_official_test.sh.
388	dispatch_debug() {
389	    local prog="$1"
390	    local out_tail="$2"
391	    local mode="${3:-inline}"
392	    local safe_name; safe_name=$(echo "$prog" | tr -c 'A-Za-z0-9_' '_' | cut -c1-30)
393	    local out_json="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.translator.json"
394	    local transcript="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.transcript.jsonl"
395	
396	    local prompt_template; prompt_template=$(cat "$ROOT/harness/debug_agent_prompt.txt")
397	    local prompt="${prompt_template//__PROG__/$prog}"
398	    prompt="${prompt//__OUTPUT__/$out_tail}"
399	    prompt="${prompt//__MODE__/$mode}"
400	
401	    export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-personal"
402	    unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
403	    export CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS="${CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-64000}"
404	
405	    claude -p \
406	        --append-system-prompt "$(cat PORTING.md)" \
407	        --allowedTools "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash(cargo build*),Bash(cargo check*),Bash(grep *),Bash(rg *),Bash(cat *),Bash(head *),Bash(tail *),Bash(wc *),Bash(find *),Bash(target/debug/lua-rs *),Bash(./harness/run_official_test.sh *)" \
408	        --permission-mode dontAsk \
409	        --output-format stream-json \
410	        --include-partial-messages \
411	        --verbose \
412	        --max-budget-usd "$PER_AGENT_BUDGET" \
413	        "$prompt" \
414	        2>>"$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.stderr" \
415	        | tee "$transcript" >/dev/null
416	
417	    jq -s 'map(select(.type == "result")) | .[-1] // {}' "$transcript" > "$out_json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}' > "$out_json"
418	    local cost; cost=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$out_json")
419	    commit_agent_changes "debug" "$prog" "$out_json"
420	    echo "$cost"
421	}
422	
423	# Extract a coarse error signature from a test's output. Used for stuck-detect:
424	# two consecutive failures with the same signature on the same program means we
425	# can't make progress on that program this session.
426	error_signature() {
427	    local out_file="$1"
428	    if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$out_file"; then
429	        grep -E "not yet implemented:" "$out_file" | head -1 \
430	            | sed -E 's/^.*not yet implemented: //' | head -c 80
431	    elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$out_file"; then
432	        grep -oE "panicked at [^:]+:[0-9]+" "$out_file" | head -1
433	    elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
434	        grep -E "^\[err\]" "$out_file" | head -1 | head -c 80
435	    else
436	        echo "unknown"
437	    fi
438	}
439	
440	# Whether $1 is in the STUCK_PROGS list.
441	is_stuck() {
442	    local p="$1"
443	    for s in "${STUCK_PROGS[@]+"${STUCK_PROGS[@]}"}"; do
444	        if [ "$s" = "$p" ]; then return 0; fi
445	    done
446	    return 1
447	}
448	
449	emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
450	emit "mega-loop start. ${#PROGS[@]} test programs."
451	emit "  MAX_OUTER=$MAX_OUTER  MAX_PER_OUTER=$MAX_PER_OUTER  PER_AGENT_BUDGET=\$$PER_AGENT_BUDGET  SESSION_BUDGET=\$$SESSION_BUDGET"
452	emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
453	
454	OUTER=0
455	TOTAL_COST=0
456	PREV_QUEUE=""
457	
458	while [ "$OUTER" -lt "$MAX_OUTER" ]; do
459	    OUTER=$((OUTER + 1))
460	    emit "─── outer round $OUTER (spent: \$$TOTAL_COST) ───"
461	
462	    # SESSION_BUDGET guard — abort cleanly before dispatching more work.
463	    budget_exceeded=$(awk -v t="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$SESSION_BUDGET" 'BEGIN { print (t >= b) ? 1 : 0 }')
464	    if [ "$budget_exceeded" = "1" ]; then
465	        emit "  SESSION_BUDGET (\$$SESSION_BUDGET) reached. Stopping cleanly."
466	        break
467	    fi
468	
469	    if ! cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>"$OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err"; then
470	        emit "  BUILD BROKEN at top of round — abort. See $OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err"
471	        head -30 "$OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err" | tee -a "$LOG"
472	        break
473	    fi
474	
475	    # ONE scan per outer round; reused for pass-count + stub-extraction.
476	    SCAN_DIR="$OUT_DIR/scan-$OUTER"
477	    run_all_programs "$SCAN_DIR"
478	
479	    pass=$(count_passing "$SCAN_DIR")
480	    emit "  scan: $pass/${#PROGS[@]} programs pass cleanly"
481	
482	    # Regression-abort: pass count should never decrease between rounds.
483	    if [ "$PREV_PASS_COUNT" -gt -1 ] && [ "$pass" -lt "$PREV_PASS_COUNT" ]; then
484	        emit "  REGRESSION: pass count went $PREV_PASS_COUNT → $pass. Aborting."
485	        break
486	    fi
487	    PREV_PASS_COUNT=$pass
488	
489	    if [ "$pass" = "${#PROGS[@]}" ]; then
490	        emit "★ all ${#PROGS[@]} programs pass. Stopping."
491	        break
492	    fi
493	
494	    queue=$(surface_scan "$SCAN_DIR")
495	    n=$(printf "%s\n" "$queue" | grep -c . || true)
496	    emit "  surface scan returned $n unique stub blockers"
497	    if [ "$n" = "0" ]; then
498	        emit "  no stub blockers — falling back to DEBUG agents on failing programs"
499	        debug_iter=0
500	        idx=0
501	        debug_pids=()
502	        debug_costs_file="$OUT_DIR/debug-costs-O$OUTER.txt"
503	        : > "$debug_costs_file"
504	        for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
505	            idx=$((idx + 1))
506	            out_file="$SCAN_DIR/prog-$idx.out"
507	            exp="${EXPECTED[$((idx-1))]}"
508	            if [ ! -f "$out_file" ]; then continue; fi
509	            failing=1
510	            if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$out_file" \
511	                && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|panicked at|^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
512	                if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
513	                    actual=$(awk '
514	                        /^\[4\/4\] Executing chunk/ { capture=1; next }
515	                        /^\[ok\] execution completed/ { capture=0 }
516	                        capture { print }
517	                    ' "$out_file")
518	                    expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
519	                    if [ "$actual" = "$expected_decoded" ]; then failing=0; fi
520	                else
521	                    failing=0
522	                fi
523	            fi
524	            if [ "$failing" = "0" ]; then continue; fi
525	            # Stuck-detect: same prog + same error signature as last attempt = skip.
526	            current_sig=$(error_signature "$out_file")
527	            sig_key="${prog}::${current_sig}"
528	            if is_stuck "$sig_key"; then
529	                emit "  skip stuck prog (sig: $current_sig): ${LABELS[$((idx-1))]:-inline}"
530	                continue
531	            fi
532	            STUCK_PROGS+=("$sig_key")
533	            debug_iter=$((debug_iter + 1))
534	            if [ "$debug_iter" -gt "$MAX_PER_OUTER" ]; then break; fi
535	            ITER=$debug_iter
536	            out_tail=$(tail -25 "$out_file")
537	            if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
538	                expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
539	                out_tail="EXPECTED OUTPUT (between [4/4] and [ok] lines):
540	$expected_decoded
541	
542	ACTUAL OUTPUT (tail):
543	$out_tail"
544	            fi
545	            label="${LABELS[$((idx-1))]:-}"
546	            while [ "${#debug_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ]; do
547	                new_pids=()
548	                for p in "${debug_pids[@]+"${debug_pids[@]}"}"; do
549	                    if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
550	                done
551	                debug_pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
552	                [ "${#debug_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ] && sleep 2
553	            done
554	            if [ -n "$label" ]; then
555	                emit "  [O$OUTER.D$debug_iter] DEBUG dispatch on file: $label [parallel]"
556	                (
557	                    cost=$(dispatch_debug "$label" "$out_tail" "file")
558	                    echo "$debug_iter $cost $label" >> "$debug_costs_file"
559	                ) &
560	            else
561	                emit "  [O$OUTER.D$debug_iter] DEBUG dispatch on prog: $prog [parallel]"
562	                (
563	                    cost=$(dispatch_debug "$prog" "$out_tail" "inline")
564	                    echo "$debug_iter $cost inline" >> "$debug_costs_file"
565	                ) &
566	            fi
567	            debug_pids+=($!)
568	        done
569	        for p in "${debug_pids[@]+"${debug_pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done
570	        while read -r iter c rest; do
571	            TOTAL_COST=$(awk -v a="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$c" 'BEGIN { printf "%.4f", a + b }')
572	            emit "  [O$OUTER.D$iter] DEBUG done. iter cost=\$$c  total=\$$TOTAL_COST"
573	        done < "$debug_costs_file"
574	        if [ "$debug_iter" = "0" ]; then
575	            emit "  no failing programs and no stubs — done."
576	            break
577	        fi
578	        continue
579	    fi
580	    printf "%s\n" "$queue" > "$QUEUE"
581	
582	    # Stuck-detect: if the queue is byte-identical to the previous round's, we're stuck.
583	    if [ "$queue" = "$PREV_QUEUE" ]; then
584	        emit "  STUCK: queue identical to previous round. Bailing."
585	        break
586	    fi
587	    PREV_QUEUE="$queue"
588	
589	    # Group the queue by file. Keep only the FIRST stub per file (the
590	    # family-aware agent will sweep siblings inside its file). This lets
591	    # us run agents on disjoint files in parallel without conflicts.
592	    declare -a seen_files=()
593	    declare -a batch_funcs=()
594	    declare -a batch_locs=()
595	    while IFS=$'\t' read -r func loc; do
596	        [ -z "$func" ] && continue
597	        [ "${#batch_funcs[@]}" -ge "$MAX_PER_OUTER" ] && break
598	        file="${loc%%:*}"
599	        already=0
600	        for sf in "${seen_files[@]+"${seen_files[@]}"}"; do
601	            if [ "$sf" = "$file" ]; then already=1; break; fi
602	        done
603	        [ "$already" = "1" ] && continue
604	        seen_files+=("$file")
605	        batch_funcs+=("$func")
606	        batch_locs+=("$loc")
607	    done <<< "$queue"
608	
609	    emit "  grouped into ${#batch_funcs[@]} disjoint-file agents (parallel cap $PARALLEL_AGENTS)"
610	    ITER=0
611	    agent_pids=()
612	    agent_costs_file="$OUT_DIR/agent-costs-O$OUTER.txt"
613	    : > "$agent_costs_file"
614	    while [ "$ITER" -lt "${#batch_funcs[@]}" ]; do
615	        # Limit concurrent agents.
616	        while [ "${#agent_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ]; do
617	            new_pids=()
618	            for p in "${agent_pids[@]+"${agent_pids[@]}"}"; do
619	                if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
620	            done
621	            agent_pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
622	            [ "${#agent_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ] && sleep 2
623	        done
624	        ITER=$((ITER + 1))
625	        func="${batch_funcs[$((ITER-1))]}"
626	        loc="${batch_locs[$((ITER-1))]}"
627	        emit "  [O$OUTER.$ITER] dispatch on '$func' @ $loc (budget \$$PER_AGENT_BUDGET) [parallel]"
628	        (
629	            cost=$(dispatch_one "$func" "$loc")
630	            echo "$ITER $cost $func" >> "$agent_costs_file"
631	        ) &
632	        agent_pids+=($!)
633	    done
634	    for p in "${agent_pids[@]+"${agent_pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done
635	
636	    # Roll up costs from this round.
637	    while read -r iter c rest; do
638	        TOTAL_COST=$(awk -v a="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$c" 'BEGIN { printf "%.4f", a + b }')
639	        emit "  [O$OUTER.$iter] agent done. iter cost=\$$c  total=\$$TOTAL_COST"
640	    done < "$agent_costs_file"
641	done
642	
643	emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
644	emit "mega-loop end. Total cost: \$$TOTAL_COST  Outer rounds: $OUTER"
645	emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# mega_loop.sh — wide-funnel driver for the lua-rs-port stub frontier.
#
# Instead of stair-stepping one test program at a time, this:
#   1. Runs N test programs in parallel-ish (sequentially with timeouts)
#   2. Collects the union of `not yet implemented:` panics
#   3. Drains the resulting stub queue, dispatching agents (one per
#      unique stub) — the agent prompt already invites family expansion,
#      so each dispatch typically clears 5-15 sibling stubs in one go
#   4. Re-runs all programs after the queue drains, finds any new
#      surfaced stubs, repeats
#   5. Stops when all programs pass OR a stuck condition fires
#
# Usage:
#   nohup ./harness/mega_loop.sh > /tmp/mega.log 2>&1 &
#
# Programs source (in priority order):
#   1. argv if given:   ./harness/mega_loop.sh prog1 prog2 ...
#   2. $TEST_PROGS_FILE env (newline-separated, # comments OK)
#   3. Built-in default list (see DEFAULT_PROGS below)

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="harness/impl"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
LOG="$OUT_DIR/mega.log"
QUEUE="$OUT_DIR/stub_queue.txt"
touch "$LOG" "$QUEUE"

MAX_OUTER=${MAX_OUTER:-20}
MAX_PER_OUTER=${MAX_PER_OUTER:-25}
PER_AGENT_BUDGET=${PER_AGENT_BUDGET:-20.00}
TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-20}
SESSION_BUDGET=${SESSION_BUDGET:-400.00}
AUTO_COMMIT=${AUTO_COMMIT:-1}
COMMIT_LOCK_DIR="$OUT_DIR/commit.lock"
PREV_PASS_COUNT=-1
declare -a STUCK_PROGS=()

# A frontier of Lua test programs. Each entry is a TSV: program\texpected_stdout.
# expected_stdout uses literal \n for newlines and \t for tabs (interpreted via printf %b).
# Empty expected_stdout = "anything non-error counts as a pass".
DEFAULT_PROGS=(
    $'print("hello")\thello'
    $'print(1+2)\t3'
    $'local x = 5; print(x)\t5'
    $'local x = 5; local y = 3; print(x+y)\t8'
    $'if 1 < 2 then print("yes") end\tyes'
    $'for i=1,3 do print(i) end\t1\n2\n3'
    $'local i = 1; while i <= 3 do print(i); i = i + 1 end\t1\n2\n3'
    $'local function f(x) return x*2 end; print(f(5))\t10'
    $'local function fact(n) if n <= 1 then return 1 else return n * fact(n-1) end end; print(fact(5))\t120'
    $'local function counter() local n = 0; return function() n = n + 1; return n end end; local c = counter(); print(c()); print(c())\t1\n2'
    $'local t = {10,20,30}; print(t[1])\t10'
    $'local t = {}; table.insert(t, "a"); print(t[1])\ta'
    $'print("hi " .. "there")\thi there'
    $'print(#"hello")\t5'
    $'print(string.upper("hello"))\tHELLO'
    $'print(string.format("%d", 42))\t42'
    $'print(math.sqrt(16))\t4.0'
    $'local ok, err = pcall(function() error("boom") end); print(ok)\tfalse'
    $'local a, b = (function() return 1, 2 end)(); print(a, b)\t1\t2'
    $'local t = {1,2,3}; local s = 0; for i=1,#t do s = s + t[i] end; print(s)\t6'
    $'local f = function(x, y) return x or y end; print(f(nil, 42))\t42'
    $'local t = {}; for i=1,3 do t[#t+1] = i*i end; for i=1,#t do print(t[i]) end\t1\n4\n9'
    $'local o = {x=10}; function o.get() return o.x end; print(o.get())\t10'
    $'local s = ""; for i=1,3 do s = s .. tostring(i) end; print(s)\t123'
    $'local function fib(n) if n < 2 then return n else return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2) end end; print(fib(10))\t55'
    # --- next wave: method syntax, varargs, ipairs/pairs, metatables, more stdlib ---
    $'local o = {x=10}; function o:get() return self.x end; print(o:get())\t10'
    $'local function sum(...) local s = 0; for _, v in ipairs({...}) do s = s + v end; return s end; print(sum(1,2,3,4,5))\t15'
    $'local t = {a=1, b=2, c=3}; local total = 0; for k,v in pairs(t) do total = total + v end; print(total)\t6'
    $'local mt = {__add = function(a, b) return {v = a.v + b.v} end}; local a = setmetatable({v=10}, mt); local b = setmetatable({v=5}, mt); print((a+b).v)\t15'
    $'print(string.sub("hello world", 7))\tworld'
    $'print(string.rep("ab", 3))\tababab'
    $'print(string.find("hello world", "world"))\t7\t11'
    $'print(math.max(1, 5, 3, 7, 2))\t7'
    $'print(math.floor(3.7))\t3'
    $'local t = {3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6}; table.sort(t); for i=1,#t do io.write(t[i]); if i < #t then io.write(",") end end; print()\t1,1,2,3,4,5,6,9'
    $'local function rev(s) local r = ""; for i = #s, 1, -1 do r = r .. string.sub(s, i, i) end; return r end; print(rev("hello"))\tolleh'
    $'local function compose(f, g) return function(x) return f(g(x)) end end; local addone = function(x) return x+1 end; local double = function(x) return x*2 end; print(compose(addone, double)(5))\t11'
    $'local function range(a, b) local t = {}; for i=a,b do t[#t+1] = i end; return t end; local r = range(1, 5); print(#r, r[1], r[5])\t5\t1\t5'
    # --- codegen gaps that block the official Lua test suite ---
    $'print(not true)\tfalse'
    $'print(not false)\ttrue'
    $'print(not nil)\ttrue'
    $'if not false then print("y") end\ty'
    $'local x = 1; if not (x == 2) then print("ne") end\tne'
    $'local r = (1==1 and 2==2 and 3==3); print(r)\ttrue'
    $'local r = (1==1 and 2==3 and 4==4); print(r)\tfalse'
    $'local function clip(x, lo, hi) if x < lo then return lo elseif x > hi then return hi else return x end end; print(clip(5,1,10), clip(-1,1,10), clip(99,1,10))\t5\t1\t10'
    $'do local i = 0; repeat i = i + 1; print(i) until i >= 3 end\t1\n2\n3'
    $'for i = 5, 1, -1 do print(i) end\t5\n4\n3\n2\n1'
    # --- official Lua 5.4 test files (sourced from reference/lua-c/testes) ---
    # No expected stdout: counted as a pass when binary exits with [ok] and no
    # panic/[err] — these tests typically self-assert with assert(...) calls.
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/tracegc.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/big.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/verybig.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/bwcoercion.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/heavy.lua'
    # These fail with a Phase-B stub blocker, so they feed the surface-scan
    # frontier and let one family-aware agent dispatch clear them.
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/goto.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/sort.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/closure.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/pm.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/bitwise.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/math.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/tpack.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/locals.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/calls.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/constructs.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/nextvar.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/utf8.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/files.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/errors.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/api.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/attrib.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/main.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/events.lua'
    '@FILE:reference/lua-c/testes/code.lua'
)

emit() {
    local ts; ts=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    echo "[$ts mega] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"
}

# Resolve which test programs to use. Each entry is TSV: prog\texpected.
# Split into two parallel arrays.
RAW=()
if [ $# -gt 0 ]; then
    RAW=("$@")
elif [ -n "${TEST_PROGS_FILE:-}" ] && [ -f "$TEST_PROGS_FILE" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [ -z "$line" ] && continue
        [[ "$line" =~ ^# ]] && continue
        RAW+=("$line")
    done < "$TEST_PROGS_FILE"
else
    RAW=("${DEFAULT_PROGS[@]}")
fi

PREAMBLE='_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

PROGS=()
EXPECTED=()
LABELS=()
for entry in "${RAW[@]}"; do
    prog="${entry%%$'\t'*}"
    rest="${entry#*$'\t'}"
    if [ "$rest" = "$entry" ]; then
        exp=""
    else
        exp="$rest"
    fi
    label=""
    if [[ "$prog" == @FILE:* ]]; then
        path="${prog#@FILE:}"
        if [ ! -f "$path" ]; then
            emit "WARN: skipping @FILE entry — not found: $path"
            continue
        fi
        label="$path"
        prog="$PREAMBLE"$'\n'"$(cat "$path")"
    fi
    PROGS+=("$prog")
    EXPECTED+=("$exp")
    LABELS+=("$label")
done

emit "loaded ${#PROGS[@]} test programs (with expected outputs)"

# Make sure binary exists.
ensure_binary() {
    cargo build -q -p lua-cli >/dev/null 2>&1
}

# Run one test program with timeout, return its output.
run_one() {
    local prog="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local bin="target/debug/lua-rs"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$bin" "$prog" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 $pid 2>/dev/null ) &
        local watcher=$!
        wait $pid 2>/dev/null
        kill $watcher 2>/dev/null
    fi
}

# How many test runs to launch in parallel. lua-rs binary is single-threaded
# and each run is small; 8-16 is a sensible cap.
PARALLEL_RUNS=${PARALLEL_RUNS:-12}
# How many agent dispatches to run concurrently. Agents on the same file
# can conflict, so we serialize same-file work; this caps the *total*
# concurrent agents across different files.
PARALLEL_AGENTS=${PARALLEL_AGENTS:-3}

# Run all programs in parallel into $1 dir; output files in $1/prog-N.out.
run_all_programs() {
    local scan_dir="$1"
    mkdir -p "$scan_dir"
    local pids=()
    local i=0
    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
        i=$((i + 1))
        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$i.out"
        while [ "${#pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_RUNS" ]; do
            local new_pids=()
            for p in "${pids[@]}"; do
                if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
            done
            pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
            [ "${#pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_RUNS" ] && sleep 0.2
        done
        ( run_one "$prog" "$out_file" || true ) &
        pids+=($!)
    done
    for p in "${pids[@]+"${pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done
}

# Surface scan: derive unique stub blockers from a populated scan dir.
PARALLEL_RUNS=${PARALLEL_RUNS:-8}
surface_scan() {
    local scan_dir="$1"
    local stub_lines=()
    local idx=0
    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
        idx=$((idx + 1))
        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$idx.out"
        [ ! -f "$out_file" ] && continue
        local stub_func; stub_func=$(grep -E "not yet implemented: [a-z0-9_-]+:" "$out_file" \
            | head -1 | sed -E 's/^.*not yet implemented: [a-z0-9_-]+: //' \
            | sed -E 's/[[:space:]]+$//')
        if [ -n "$stub_func" ]; then
            local stub_loc; stub_loc=$(grep -oE "panicked at [^:]+:[0-9]+:[0-9]+" "$out_file" \
                | head -1 | sed 's/^panicked at //')
            stub_lines+=("$stub_func"$'\t'"$stub_loc")
        fi
    done
    if [ ${#stub_lines[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
        return 0
    fi
    printf "%s\n" "${stub_lines[@]}" | sort -u -t$'\t' -k1,1
}

# Run all programs in parallel, return how many produce expected output.
# A program passes only when:
#   (a) exits cleanly ([ok] line + no panic + no [err]), AND
#   (b) if EXPECTED is non-empty, stdout (post-[4/4]) matches exactly.
count_passing() {
    local scan_dir="$1"
    local pass=0
    local idx=0
    for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
        idx=$((idx + 1))
        local out_file="$scan_dir/prog-$idx.out"
        local exp="${EXPECTED[$((idx-1))]}"
        [ ! -f "$out_file" ] && continue
        if ! grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$out_file" \
            || grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$out_file" \
            || grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$out_file" \
            || grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
            continue
        fi
        if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
            local actual; actual=$(awk '
                /^\[4\/4\] Executing chunk/ { capture=1; next }
                /^\[ok\] execution completed/ { capture=0 }
                capture { print }
            ' "$out_file")
            local expected_decoded; expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
            if [ "$actual" = "$expected_decoded" ]; then
                pass=$((pass + 1))
            fi
        else
            pass=$((pass + 1))
        fi
    done
    echo "$pass"
}

# Commit any pending changes under crates/ as one commit attributed to the
# named agent. Serialized via mkdir-lock so parallel agents don't race.
# Args: kind (impl|debug), target (func or prog), out_json (translator.json).
commit_agent_changes() {
    [ "$AUTO_COMMIT" != "1" ] && return 0
    local kind="$1"
    local target="$2"
    local out_json="$3"

    local lock_tries=0
    while ! mkdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null; do
        lock_tries=$((lock_tries + 1))
        if [ "$lock_tries" -gt 120 ]; then
            emit "  commit-lock timeout, skipping commit for $kind/$target"
            return 0
        fi
        sleep 0.5
    done

    if [ -z "$(git status --porcelain crates/ 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        rmdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi

    local result_text; result_text=$(jq -r '.result // ""' "$out_json" 2>/dev/null)
    local title_target; title_target=$(echo "$target" | tr '\n' ' ' | cut -c1-50)
    local subject="agent ${kind}: ${title_target}"
    local body_file="$OUT_DIR/.commit-body.tmp"
    {
        echo "$subject"
        echo
        printf "%s\n" "$result_text" | head -50
    } > "$body_file"

    git add crates/ >/dev/null 2>&1
    if git commit -F "$body_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local sha; sha=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
        emit "  → committed $sha (${kind}/${title_target})"
    else
        emit "  → no commit (probably nothing in crates/)"
    fi
    rm -f "$body_file"
    rmdir "$COMMIT_LOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null
}

# Dispatch a family-aware implement agent for one stub (the prompt
# already invites the agent to handle siblings while in context).
dispatch_one() {
    local func="$1"
    local loc="${2:-}"
    local safe_name; safe_name=$(echo "$func" | tr -c 'A-Za-z0-9_' '_' | cut -c1-40)
    local out_json="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.translator.json"
    local transcript="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.transcript.jsonl"

    local prompt_template; prompt_template=$(cat "$ROOT/harness/family_agent_prompt.txt")
    local prompt="${prompt_template//__FUNC__/$func}"
    prompt="${prompt//__LOC__/$loc}"

    export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-personal"
    unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
    export CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS="${CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-64000}"
    export CLAUDE_TARGET_RS_FILE="crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs"

    claude -p \
        --append-system-prompt "$(cat PORTING.md)" \
        --allowedTools "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash(cargo build*),Bash(cargo check*),Bash(grep *),Bash(rg *),Bash(cat *),Bash(head *),Bash(tail *),Bash(wc *),Bash(find *)" \
        --permission-mode dontAsk \
        --output-format stream-json \
        --include-partial-messages \
        --verbose \
        --max-budget-usd "$PER_AGENT_BUDGET" \
        "$prompt" \
        2>>"$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-$ITER-$safe_name.stderr" \
        | tee "$transcript" >/dev/null

    jq -s 'map(select(.type == "result")) | .[-1] // {}' "$transcript" > "$out_json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}' > "$out_json"
    local cost; cost=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$out_json")
    commit_agent_changes "impl" "$func" "$out_json"
    echo "$cost"
}

# Dispatch a DEBUG agent for a failing test program (real error, not a stub).
# Mode "inline": $prog is Lua source. Mode "file": $prog is a path to a .lua file
# that the agent should run via ./harness/run_official_test.sh.
dispatch_debug() {
    local prog="$1"
    local out_tail="$2"
    local mode="${3:-inline}"
    local safe_name; safe_name=$(echo "$prog" | tr -c 'A-Za-z0-9_' '_' | cut -c1-30)
    local out_json="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.translator.json"
    local transcript="$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.transcript.jsonl"

    local prompt_template; prompt_template=$(cat "$ROOT/harness/debug_agent_prompt.txt")
    local prompt="${prompt_template//__PROG__/$prog}"
    prompt="${prompt//__OUTPUT__/$out_tail}"
    prompt="${prompt//__MODE__/$mode}"

    export CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-personal"
    unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
    export CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS="${CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-64000}"

    claude -p \
        --append-system-prompt "$(cat PORTING.md)" \
        --allowedTools "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash(cargo build*),Bash(cargo check*),Bash(grep *),Bash(rg *),Bash(cat *),Bash(head *),Bash(tail *),Bash(wc *),Bash(find *),Bash(target/debug/lua-rs *),Bash(./harness/run_official_test.sh *)" \
        --permission-mode dontAsk \
        --output-format stream-json \
        --include-partial-messages \
        --verbose \
        --max-budget-usd "$PER_AGENT_BUDGET" \
        "$prompt" \
        2>>"$OUT_DIR/mega-O$OUTER-D$ITER-$safe_name.stderr" \
        | tee "$transcript" >/dev/null

    jq -s 'map(select(.type == "result")) | .[-1] // {}' "$transcript" > "$out_json" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}' > "$out_json"
    local cost; cost=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$out_json")
    commit_agent_changes "debug" "$prog" "$out_json"
    echo "$cost"
}

# Extract a coarse error signature from a test's output. Used for stuck-detect:
# two consecutive failures with the same signature on the same program means we
# can't make progress on that program this session.
error_signature() {
    local out_file="$1"
    if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$out_file"; then
        grep -E "not yet implemented:" "$out_file" | head -1 \
            | sed -E 's/^.*not yet implemented: //' | head -c 80
    elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$out_file"; then
        grep -oE "panicked at [^:]+:[0-9]+" "$out_file" | head -1
    elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
        grep -E "^\[err\]" "$out_file" | head -1 | head -c 80
    else
        echo "unknown"
    fi
}

# Whether $1 is in the STUCK_PROGS list.
is_stuck() {
    local p="$1"
    for s in "${STUCK_PROGS[@]+"${STUCK_PROGS[@]}"}"; do
        if [ "$s" = "$p" ]; then return 0; fi
    done
    return 1
}

emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
emit "mega-loop start. ${#PROGS[@]} test programs."
emit "  MAX_OUTER=$MAX_OUTER  MAX_PER_OUTER=$MAX_PER_OUTER  PER_AGENT_BUDGET=\$$PER_AGENT_BUDGET  SESSION_BUDGET=\$$SESSION_BUDGET"
emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

OUTER=0
TOTAL_COST=0
PREV_QUEUE=""

while [ "$OUTER" -lt "$MAX_OUTER" ]; do
    OUTER=$((OUTER + 1))
    emit "─── outer round $OUTER (spent: \$$TOTAL_COST) ───"

    # SESSION_BUDGET guard — abort cleanly before dispatching more work.
    budget_exceeded=$(awk -v t="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$SESSION_BUDGET" 'BEGIN { print (t >= b) ? 1 : 0 }')
    if [ "$budget_exceeded" = "1" ]; then
        emit "  SESSION_BUDGET (\$$SESSION_BUDGET) reached. Stopping cleanly."
        break
    fi

    if ! cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>"$OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err"; then
        emit "  BUILD BROKEN at top of round — abort. See $OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err"
        head -30 "$OUT_DIR/build-O$OUTER.err" | tee -a "$LOG"
        break
    fi

    # ONE scan per outer round; reused for pass-count + stub-extraction.
    SCAN_DIR="$OUT_DIR/scan-$OUTER"
    run_all_programs "$SCAN_DIR"

    pass=$(count_passing "$SCAN_DIR")
    emit "  scan: $pass/${#PROGS[@]} programs pass cleanly"

    # Regression-abort: pass count should never decrease between rounds.
    if [ "$PREV_PASS_COUNT" -gt -1 ] && [ "$pass" -lt "$PREV_PASS_COUNT" ]; then
        emit "  REGRESSION: pass count went $PREV_PASS_COUNT → $pass. Aborting."
        break
    fi
    PREV_PASS_COUNT=$pass

    if [ "$pass" = "${#PROGS[@]}" ]; then
        emit "★ all ${#PROGS[@]} programs pass. Stopping."
        break
    fi

    queue=$(surface_scan "$SCAN_DIR")
    n=$(printf "%s\n" "$queue" | grep -c . || true)
    emit "  surface scan returned $n unique stub blockers"
    if [ "$n" = "0" ]; then
        emit "  no stub blockers — falling back to DEBUG agents on failing programs"
        debug_iter=0
        idx=0
        debug_pids=()
        debug_costs_file="$OUT_DIR/debug-costs-O$OUTER.txt"
        : > "$debug_costs_file"
        for prog in "${PROGS[@]}"; do
            idx=$((idx + 1))
            out_file="$SCAN_DIR/prog-$idx.out"
            exp="${EXPECTED[$((idx-1))]}"
            if [ ! -f "$out_file" ]; then continue; fi
            failing=1
            if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$out_file" \
                && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|panicked at|^\[err\]" "$out_file"; then
                if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
                    actual=$(awk '
                        /^\[4\/4\] Executing chunk/ { capture=1; next }
                        /^\[ok\] execution completed/ { capture=0 }
                        capture { print }
                    ' "$out_file")
                    expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
                    if [ "$actual" = "$expected_decoded" ]; then failing=0; fi
                else
                    failing=0
                fi
            fi
            if [ "$failing" = "0" ]; then continue; fi
            # Stuck-detect: same prog + same error signature as last attempt = skip.
            current_sig=$(error_signature "$out_file")
            sig_key="${prog}::${current_sig}"
            if is_stuck "$sig_key"; then
                emit "  skip stuck prog (sig: $current_sig): ${LABELS[$((idx-1))]:-inline}"
                continue
            fi
            STUCK_PROGS+=("$sig_key")
            debug_iter=$((debug_iter + 1))
            if [ "$debug_iter" -gt "$MAX_PER_OUTER" ]; then break; fi
            ITER=$debug_iter
            out_tail=$(tail -25 "$out_file")
            if [ -n "$exp" ]; then
                expected_decoded=$(printf "%b" "$exp")
                out_tail="EXPECTED OUTPUT (between [4/4] and [ok] lines):
$expected_decoded

ACTUAL OUTPUT (tail):
$out_tail"
            fi
            label="${LABELS[$((idx-1))]:-}"
            while [ "${#debug_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ]; do
                new_pids=()
                for p in "${debug_pids[@]+"${debug_pids[@]}"}"; do
                    if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
                done
                debug_pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
                [ "${#debug_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ] && sleep 2
            done
            if [ -n "$label" ]; then
                emit "  [O$OUTER.D$debug_iter] DEBUG dispatch on file: $label [parallel]"
                (
                    cost=$(dispatch_debug "$label" "$out_tail" "file")
                    echo "$debug_iter $cost $label" >> "$debug_costs_file"
                ) &
            else
                emit "  [O$OUTER.D$debug_iter] DEBUG dispatch on prog: $prog [parallel]"
                (
                    cost=$(dispatch_debug "$prog" "$out_tail" "inline")
                    echo "$debug_iter $cost inline" >> "$debug_costs_file"
                ) &
            fi
            debug_pids+=($!)
        done
        for p in "${debug_pids[@]+"${debug_pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done
        while read -r iter c rest; do
            TOTAL_COST=$(awk -v a="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$c" 'BEGIN { printf "%.4f", a + b }')
            emit "  [O$OUTER.D$iter] DEBUG done. iter cost=\$$c  total=\$$TOTAL_COST"
        done < "$debug_costs_file"
        if [ "$debug_iter" = "0" ]; then
            emit "  no failing programs and no stubs — done."
            break
        fi
        continue
    fi
    printf "%s\n" "$queue" > "$QUEUE"

    # Stuck-detect: if the queue is byte-identical to the previous round's, we're stuck.
    if [ "$queue" = "$PREV_QUEUE" ]; then
        emit "  STUCK: queue identical to previous round. Bailing."
        break
    fi
    PREV_QUEUE="$queue"

    # Group the queue by file. Keep only the FIRST stub per file (the
    # family-aware agent will sweep siblings inside its file). This lets
    # us run agents on disjoint files in parallel without conflicts.
    declare -a seen_files=()
    declare -a batch_funcs=()
    declare -a batch_locs=()
    while IFS=$'\t' read -r func loc; do
        [ -z "$func" ] && continue
        [ "${#batch_funcs[@]}" -ge "$MAX_PER_OUTER" ] && break
        file="${loc%%:*}"
        already=0
        for sf in "${seen_files[@]+"${seen_files[@]}"}"; do
            if [ "$sf" = "$file" ]; then already=1; break; fi
        done
        [ "$already" = "1" ] && continue
        seen_files+=("$file")
        batch_funcs+=("$func")
        batch_locs+=("$loc")
    done <<< "$queue"

    emit "  grouped into ${#batch_funcs[@]} disjoint-file agents (parallel cap $PARALLEL_AGENTS)"
    ITER=0
    agent_pids=()
    agent_costs_file="$OUT_DIR/agent-costs-O$OUTER.txt"
    : > "$agent_costs_file"
    while [ "$ITER" -lt "${#batch_funcs[@]}" ]; do
        # Limit concurrent agents.
        while [ "${#agent_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ]; do
            new_pids=()
            for p in "${agent_pids[@]+"${agent_pids[@]}"}"; do
                if kill -0 "$p" 2>/dev/null; then new_pids+=("$p"); fi
            done
            agent_pids=("${new_pids[@]+"${new_pids[@]}"}")
            [ "${#agent_pids[@]}" -ge "$PARALLEL_AGENTS" ] && sleep 2
        done
        ITER=$((ITER + 1))
        func="${batch_funcs[$((ITER-1))]}"
        loc="${batch_locs[$((ITER-1))]}"
        emit "  [O$OUTER.$ITER] dispatch on '$func' @ $loc (budget \$$PER_AGENT_BUDGET) [parallel]"
        (
            cost=$(dispatch_one "$func" "$loc")
            echo "$ITER $cost $func" >> "$agent_costs_file"
        ) &
        agent_pids+=($!)
    done
    for p in "${agent_pids[@]+"${agent_pids[@]}"}"; do wait "$p" 2>/dev/null || true; done

    # Roll up costs from this round.
    while read -r iter c rest; do
        TOTAL_COST=$(awk -v a="$TOTAL_COST" -v b="$c" 'BEGIN { printf "%.4f", a + b }')
        emit "  [O$OUTER.$iter] agent done. iter cost=\$$c  total=\$$TOTAL_COST"
    done < "$agent_costs_file"
done

emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
emit "mega-loop end. Total cost: \$$TOTAL_COST  Outer rounds: $OUTER"
emit "═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

**Root cause**: Two combined bugs blocked literals.lua at line 231 (`assert(sd == s1 and getadd(sd) ~= a1)`):

1. `string.format("%p", v)` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1691` returned the literal placeholder `"(ptr)"` for every value, so `getadd(sd) ~= a1` was always `"(ptr)" ~= "(ptr)"` → false.
2. `intern_or_create_str` in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1391` interned strings of every length, so the runtime concat `"0123…" .. "01234…"` reused the same `LuaString` `Rc` as the same-content literal `s1` instead of producing a fresh long string (Lua 5.4's `luaS_newlstr` semantics: short interned, long always fresh).

**Fix**:
1. `%p` now formats `lua_vm::api::to_pointer(state, arg)` as `0x{:x}` — giving each `GcRef`'s identity-address a unique hex rendering.
2. `intern_or_create_str` now mirrors `luaS_newlstr`: `len ≤ 40` interns globally, `> 40` allocates a fresh `LuaString` via `GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(...))`, bypassing `interned_lt`.

**New state**: The literals test now executes through ~5 `+` checkpoints (lines 143, 157, 163, 172, 203) before panicking on the deliberate `coroutine.resume` stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:129` — i.e., the explicit Phase-A–D coroutine stub per PORTING.md §2 #6, which is outside the scope of this bug. The original `:18: ""` failure is gone.

All temporary `eprintln` instrumentation was removed; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).
